scope: SYBEORG only; Radi / House of Meroe excluded

discoveryPool.business: Dave Seydel Auto and Truck Repair Inc.

publicListingSignals: Nominatim/OSM listing showed no website field
selectedLeads.diagnosis: Directory-only first impression leaks specialty radiator/heater searchers before they see a clear call/email path or owned service context.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Minimum-useful first website / specialty landing page with mobile tap-to-call and tap-to-email.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Verified email plus a highly specific radiator/heater service angle makes the sample feel personal and useful.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: Old-school radiator ticket + temperature-gauge service counter; not a generic auto hero or fake-review page.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA passed: polished and readable old-school radiator-ticket/gauge concept with strong contact CTA and no layout breakage. Notes: proposal/internal language is intentional for mockup review; mobile responsiveness still worth spot-checking before final launch.

draft.subject: quick radiator page idea for Tim's Auto Services
draft.body:
Hi Tim's Auto Services team — I was looking at Lincoln auto repair listings and noticed the public listing I checked had your phone/email, but I did not see an owned website field attached.

For a radiator/heater repair shop, that can make high-intent searchers rely on a directory instead of seeing a simple “call now / describe the issue” path.

I put together a no-obligation sample concept showing how a small page could make the radiator/heater specialty and contact route clearer on mobile:
https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/tims-auto-services-radiator-repair/

It is just a draft concept — I avoided fake reviews, certifications, warranties, or owner claims. Worth polishing this up with your real photos/services, or should I leave it alone?

Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: Nominatim/OSM listing showed no website field
selectedLeads.diagnosis: HVAC searches need quick call routing and seasonal service context; without an owned page, the listing has no controlled campaign destination.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Phone-first HVAC landing page with cooling/heating/tune-up/estimate routing.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: No-site signal plus HVAC urgency makes a first-page offer immediately understandable.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: Thermostat/dial comfort dispatch design with issue routes; no dark contractor hero/right quote form pattern.
unverifiedClaimsAvoided: specific services beyond generic HVAC lanes
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA passed: polished, readable thermostat/comfort-dispatch design with no obvious layout breakage and clear phone-first CTA. Notes: concept/review language is intentionally explicit; real business proof/photos needed before launch.

draft.body: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was reviewing Lincoln HVAC listings and noticed the public listing I checked had Custom Cooling & Heating’s phone number but I did not see a website attached. I made a small no-obligation sample page showing a mobile call-first path for cooling/heating calls. If someone handles the website or marketing decisions, what is the best way to send it over for review? And if this is not useful, just tell me no and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: direct fetch returned HTTP 403 Cloudflare Attention Required
selectedLeads.diagnosis: Automated/mobile-like access friction may block some users, bots, ad reviewers, or embedded browsers before they reach the homepage.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Helpful-access alert plus short mobile conversion audit.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Helpful bug alert is low-pressure and specific.

draft.body: Hi Holt team — quick helpful note from a local site audit. When I checked holtplumbingandheating.com today, my direct fetch hit a Cloudflare “Attention Required” page instead of the homepage. It may be intentional security and may work fine for most browsers, but it can sometimes block ad reviewers, embedded browsers, or some mobile visitors. No pitch pressure — I just wanted to flag it. If useful, I can send over the exact check plus a short mobile conversion note. Worth sending, or should I leave it alone? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: homepage title uses generic 888-number call phrase
selectedLeads.diagnosis: A high-intent roofing page should feel local and campaign-specific; current title appears generic and may split local call trust.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Local Omaha storm/roof repair landing page using verified local number and real photos/services.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Specific local-number/ad-readiness angle avoids a generic redesign pitch.

draft.body: Hi Makani Roofing team — I noticed a small conversion detail while checking Omaha roofing pages: your public listing shows a local Omaha number, but the homepage title I saw says “Call (888) 602-1585.” That may be intentional, but for storm/roof-repair searchers a local-number landing page can feel more direct and usually works better for paid ads. I did not want to make assumptions or send a generic redesign pitch. If useful, I can mock up a focused Omaha storm/roof repair landing page using only your verified photos/services. Worth polishing that idea, or should I leave it alone? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: first HTML pass exposed no normal title/tel/form/mailto
selectedLeads.diagnosis: Storm/tree-service visitors need immediate job-type and call routing; heavy template pages can under-serve urgent mobile visitors.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Storm cleanup / hazardous limb landing page with tap-to-call and real photo proof if provided.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Seasonal urgency and service-page improvement are more compelling than a full redesign pitch.

draft.body: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was reviewing Iowa City tree-service pages and had a small idea for General Tree Service: a focused storm cleanup / hazardous limb page that puts the call button and job type first on mobile. I have not sent anything and did not want to assume. Who is the best person to send a one-page sample concept to for review? If it is not relevant, just say no and I will not follow up.
